# LLM Integration Requirements Checklist: Web UI Graph Browser

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-006 LLM integration requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable for graph-grounded browser chat over SPEC-018.
**Created**: 2026-07-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are browser chat requirements explicitly routed through the local backend and SPEC-018 rather than a browser-side provider SDK or separate provider implementation? [Completeness, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-041]
- [x] CHK002 Are SPEC-018 activation modes represented for endpoint-active, agent-pending, dormant, misconfigured, endpoint-fallback, rate-limited, unavailable, and error states? [Completeness, Spec FR-021, Spec FR-044, Data Model ChatStatus]
- [x] CHK003 Are chat generation response states complete for endpoint answer, fallback, pending bundle, disabled, dormant, misconfigured, rate-limited, and error paths? [Completeness, Spec FR-036, Spec FR-044, Data Model ChatResponse]
- [x] CHK004 Are SPEC-018 generation result mappings defined for endpoint answers, pending bundles with fallback text and opaque handles, and fallback results? [Resolved, Spec FR-041, Data Model ChatResponse, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK005 Are graph-context assembly responsibilities assigned to the backend rather than the browser? [Completeness, Spec FR-042, Data Model ChatRequest, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK006 Are chat request payload requirements limited to repository id, user prompt, selected symbol or view hints, and bounded context hints? [Completeness, Spec FR-042, Data Model ChatRequest]
- [x] CHK007 Are context-boundary and truncation metadata required with answers or fallbacks so chat results do not imply complete graph context? [Completeness, Spec FR-022, Spec FR-042, Data Model ChatContextBoundary]
- [x] CHK008 Are agent-bundle redemption requirements represented with opaque handles and explicit completed, pending, missing, and error states? [Completeness, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md, src/llm/agent-bundle.ts]
- [x] CHK009 Are dormant and misconfigured paths specified as honest UI states that do not ask the browser for provider keys? [Completeness, Spec FR-021, Spec FR-044, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK010 Is the provider-secret boundary explicit about excluding provider URL, model, API key, provider bearer token, raw provider response bodies, and secret surrogates from browser payloads, persisted web state, logs, and rendered UI? [Clarity, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-043, Data Model ChatStatus]
- [x] CHK011 Are safe activation variables limited to environment variable names rather than values? [Clarity, Data Model ChatStatus, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK012 Is fallback text described as safe browser-renderable text rather than evidence that a provider call succeeded? [Resolved, Spec FR-041, Data Model ChatResponse, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK013 Are pending-bundle handles described as opaque and backend-validated against containment rules? [Clarity, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md, src/llm/agent-bundle.ts]
- [x] CHK014 Are disabled, dormant, misconfigured, rate-limited, and endpoint-fallback labels clear enough to avoid conflating configuration absence with provider failure? [Clarity, Spec FR-021, Spec FR-044, Data Model ChatStatus]
- [x] CHK015 Are provider failure and bundle-emission failure requirements clear about degrading through SPEC-018 fallback without exposing provider or filesystem internals? [Resolved, Spec FR-041, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK016 Do LLM integration requirements align with the local-first runtime constraint that the browser makes no direct provider requests or external runtime connections beyond the local backend? [Consistency, Spec FR-023, Spec FR-047, SC-007, SC-008]
- [x] CHK017 Do chat adapter requirements align with SPEC-018 `generate()` behavior for endpoint, agent, dormant, misconfigured, and fallback paths? [Resolved, Spec FR-041, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md, src/llm/generate.ts]
- [x] CHK018 Do chat status requirements align with SPEC-018 config resolution for endpoint, agent, misconfigured, and dormant outcomes? [Consistency, Data Model ChatStatus, src/llm/config.ts]
- [x] CHK019 Do chat request requirements align with the rule that the browser sends no raw source bundles, provider prompts, provider config, or provider credentials? [Consistency, Spec FR-042, Spec FR-043, Data Model ChatRequest]
- [x] CHK020 Do chat response requirements align with API error-envelope requirements rather than introducing a browser-only LLM error protocol? [Consistency, Spec FR-046, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK021 Do graph-grounding requirements align with the selected repository and selected symbol/view context reset rules? [Consistency, Spec FR-032, Spec FR-033, Spec FR-042]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK022 Are secret-boundary outcomes measurable through browser network inspection across configured, disabled, and misconfigured states? [Measurability, SC-007, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md Acceptance Checks]
- [x] CHK023 Are no-direct-provider-call outcomes measurable as part of offline/no-external-runtime validation? [Measurability, SC-008, Spec FR-023, Spec FR-047]
- [x] CHK024 Are chat state outcomes measurable for dormant, misconfigured, endpoint-active, endpoint-fallback, pending-bundle, completed-bundle, rate-limited, and backend-error cases? [Measurability, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md Acceptance Checks]
- [x] CHK025 Are provider-config serialization checks measurable enough to prevent accidental exposure of endpoint URLs, model names, API keys, provider bearer tokens, and raw provider response bodies? [Measurability, Spec FR-043, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md Acceptance Checks]
- [x] CHK026 Are graph context-boundary outcomes measurable for included symbols/files, applied limits, truncation, and fallback explanation? [Measurability, Data Model ChatContextBoundary, Spec FR-022]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK027 Are primary chat scenarios covered for configured endpoint answers, selected-repository context, selected-symbol context, and visible context boundaries? [Coverage, Spec US7, Spec FR-020-FR-022, Spec FR-042]
- [x] CHK028 Are alternate chat scenarios covered for agent pending bundles, completed bundle redemption, fallback text, disabled state, dormant state, and endpoint fallback? [Coverage, Spec FR-021, Spec FR-044, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK029 Are exception scenarios covered for misconfiguration, rate limiting, unknown repo, unknown bundle, unauthorized non-loopback access, provider failure, bundle-emission failure, and backend faults? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-036, Spec FR-044, Spec FR-046]
- [x] CHK030 Are repository-switching scenarios covered so previous chat context cannot remain attached to the wrong repo after a repo change? [Coverage, Spec FR-032, Spec FR-042]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are SPEC-018 dependencies traceable to the existing config, generation, and agent-bundle seams instead of introducing new browser-owned LLM behavior? [Traceability, Spec Assumptions, Research R4, src/llm/config.ts, src/llm/generate.ts, src/llm/agent-bundle.ts]
- [x] CHK032 Are backend-owned provider calls traceable to `CODEGRAPH_LLM_*` configuration while browser-visible payloads remain redaction-safe? [Traceability, Spec FR-043, Data Model ChatStatus, src/llm/config.ts]
- [x] CHK033 Are clean-room parity requirements traceable so GitNexus-style browser AI chat is matched only at behavior level through CodeGraph-owned SPEC-018 semantics? [Traceability, Spec FR-025, Spec FR-026, Spec FR-053, Research R3, Research R4]

## Notes

- Checklist focus: SPEC-018 config modes, generation result mapping, agent-bundle handles, graph context boundaries, provider-secret redaction, fallback behavior, and browser network boundaries.
- Depth: Standard reviewer gate for Phase 4 LLM Integration requirements quality.
- Initial run 2026-07-15: CHK004, CHK012, CHK015, and CHK017 required spec, data-model, and chat-adapter remediation for explicit SPEC-018 result mapping.
- Rerun 2026-07-15: all LLM Integration requirement gaps are resolved by FR-041, ChatResponse validation rules, and the chat adapter SPEC-018 result-mapping section.
